title: Moving items with subitems to another board via automation doesn't carry subitem's columns
excerpt:
Hi, 
When I try to use automation to move an item (with all of its subitems) to another board the item and subitems are created in the other board but the subitems columns matching the same structure and name are left em…

posts.author: nsc (Nir Schneider)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,
When I try to use automation to move an item (with all of its subitems) to another board the item and subitems are created in the other board but the subitems columns matching the same structure and name are left empty. Also, there is no mapping columns for the subitems in the automation setup like there is when moving items manually.

11148×832 47.2 KB

2590×604 26.7 KB

posts.author: Ozi (Oz Israel)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Nir,

Make sure the columns names are identical and try again.

Hope this helps,
Oz

posts.author: nsc (Nir Schneider)
posts.cookedHtml:
the columns names and types are precisely the same and still nothing for the subitems…
It’s like the automation move command doesn’t recognize any mapping for subitems - the name of the subitems are moved but without the content of the columns.
Only when I do the move command manually I get the mapping for the subitems

Screenshot 2024-04-24 132927794×298 16.8 KB

posts.author: Ozi (Oz Israel)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Nir,

What is the automation? “When status changed to something move item to board”?
I tested this automation and it seems the item is moved with its subitem, including all the subitem’s content.
In the automations you can not map certain column types and subitem columns, but the system is moving all item’s and subitem’s columns, even those who didn’t been mapped, as long as the column names and types are identical in the item and subitem level.

Maybe you can try reaching monday.com support.

Regards,
Oz

posts.author: nsc (Nir Schneider)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank for your help - I actually found out that it actually does copy the data but only shows it when I refresh the web browser so yes it is working thankfully.

posts.author: StefJ (Stefania)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Dears,

I need your help, the automation that should move the item with its subitems, does not keep informations on the subitem’s columns.

Screenshot 2024-06-21 alle 12.23.041075×706 54.2 KB

and I cannot map this subitems columns in the automation

posts.author: BenATawardco (Benjamin Brown)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi! I have been using your app but when I use this specific automation it scrambles the information on status columns and sometimes misses transferring some of the sub items. Is there something I can do to get a fix for this bug?

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @BenATawardco can you reach out to me and share with me the details?
I’m available at daniel@fantasymedia.io

posts.author: rriker (Rusty Riker)
posts.cookedHtml: Has this recipe been removed from the app? I’ve read through all of them 3 times now and I don’t see this one

posts.author: edarling85 (Wild 6 | Eric Darling)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m not sure, but I still use it and it’s still available to me in the sub item automations app

posts.author: kevinfantasymedia (Kevin - Fantasy Media LTD)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @rriker @edarling85

That automation is still available in the Subitem Automations app. You can find it in Monday.com by searching for the words “with with its subitems” – it’s a typo error, I know, but the typo is actually a good thing because it makes it easier to find. There’s no other automation like that.
